title: /kernel
summary_text: For a focus-less `/kernel` dry-run, the minimal pre-implementation bootstrap sequence is: 1. Parse `/kernel` with empty `FOCUS`, reframe the request as Kernel work, and defer real implementation. 2. Launch 6+ concurrent lanes immediately, with explicit live labels/roles (don’t count “planned” or failed lanes). At minimum, include Codex sovereign + GLM + one specialist + execution/verification/monitoring lanes so total active lanes reaches 6. 3. Activate external voices: run `kglm` (or equivalent) and at least the healthiest specialist candidate(s) (`gemini-cli`/`cursor-cli`/`copilot-cli`) and record live evidence. 4. Decide run shape from live probe results: - `normal` when `codex + glm + specialist` is live. - `degraded-allowed` only if `glm` has failed twice in the same run and two specialists are live (or glm is unavailable at bootstrap with two specialists, per the prompt’s bootstrap allowance). 5. Perform the pre-implementation checks (context gather, risk/verification lane prep, plan + revision), still before editing/reverse changes. 6. Write bootstrap receipt before first acknowledgement, then only acknowledge with the required 4-line preamble and 6+ active lane manifest entries. Regarding `BLOCKED`: it is consistent (and required) to report `BLOCKED` if live probes cannot establish either valid normal shape or valid degraded-allowed shape. In plain terms, if external voice availability leaves you without a recoverable `glm` + specialist normal path and also without two specialist lanes for degraded-allowed, you must fail closed rather than claim orchestration is active.
